description: Context-aware integration intelligence: lightweight or full deep dive based on who is setting it up, type-specific protocols for five integration categories, proactive quirk solving, and a Python sync script + named SQL query library instead of a compiled CLI.

installs.manual-merge-note: Merge the contents of behavior-snippet.md into the session routines section of /agent/user.md, after the existing session-open routine. The sentinel comments mark the block boundaries.

changelog.notes: Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Fix 6 gaps found in first client test (AppLovin): ambiguous-type classification rule, secret run permission check after builder, brain/integrations/README.md stub at install, usage-patterns.md stub at onboarding, behavior snippet merge guidance, fetch-size note for large skill files.

Replaced Go CLI factory with Python sync script + named SQL query library. Agent runs everything directly — no compilation. Saved SQL queries are more flexible than CLI commands for agent-driven workflows.

Complete onboarding redesign: four-phase model with lightweight/deep-dive path selection, integration type taxonomy (5 types), type-specific protocols, and proactive quirk-solving philosophy. Default path is lightweight with offer to go deeper.

Community intelligence sweep and live spot-check added to onboarding. Behavioral detective work and practical-guide.md added to activation. Quirks pre-populated from community research before first use.
Initial release. Three-stage connection flow (onboarding + activation + context sweep), CLI factory with quirks and usage feedback, three-layer retrieval model, never-twice quirks guarantee.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.
